english: separable, not to be distinguished, quite the same, identical Glr. and elsewh.; blá-mar (bla mar) dbyér-med (dbyer med) prob.: identical with a Lama; esp. in the higher philosophy in reference to the impossibility of distinguishing between good and evil (!).
Schr.: magnitude, size, dimensions, so perh. where dprál-bai (dpral ba'i) dbyes (dbyes) čé (che) is mentioned as a characteristic of beauty.
v. p'rag (phrag), intermediate space, interstice; ravine, glen, defile, C.; Sch. also: vise, handvise.
v. _obrád-pa ('brad pa).
v. _obráb-pa ('brab pa).
v. _obrál-ba ('bral ba).
v. _obrí-ba ('bri ba).
Sch.; Lex. dbre-btsog (dbre btsog) dirt, filth.
v. p'róg-pa (phrog pa).

Sch.: 'seizure, distraint'; or rather the liability of paying higher interest, payment not having been made at the appointed time; _oba-gan ('ba' gan), _oba-gan-yig ('ba' gan yig) warrant for thus proceeding against a debtor C.
Wdñ.; Sch.: lees from distilling brandy.
magician, sorcerer, conjurer; _obá-mo ('ba' mo) sorceress, witch Cs., W.
1. to bleat, W. *ba tún-če* (ba tun che). - 2. to bring, to carry, _obā-šog ('ba' shog) bring it hither! Sik., _oba-soñ ('ba' song) take it there! - 3. to commit adultery C.
Cs. = p'ug-pa (phug pa), hole, cave, cavern, brág-gi (brag gi) cleft in a rock, grotto; _obá-bo-čan ('ba' bo can) hollow, excavated.
a kind of cake, baked of parched rice or maize meal, frequently eaten with the tea C.

B. only, solely, alone, bdag (bdag) _obá-žig ('ba' zhig) tăr-ro (thar ro) I alone escaped Dzl.; rkáñ-pa (rkang pa) _obá-žig ('ba' zhig) the foot alone (appeared party-coloured) Dzl.; blón-po (blon po) de (de) _obá-žig-gi ('ba' zhig gi) čúñ-ma (chung ma) only this officer's wife Dzl.; mere, nothing but, yser (gser) dañ (dang) dñul (dngul) _oba-žig-gis ('ba' zhig gis) gañ (gang) Sbh.

1. mask, guise, disguise; cf. also sub sgo-lo (sgo lo). - 2. imitation, effigy, likeness, figure, _odra-_obág ('dra 'bag) resp. sku-_obag (sku 'bag), žal-_obág (zhal 'bag) id.; _odra-_obag-gyon-mí ('dra 'bag gyon mi) masked persons Pth - _obag-čam ('bag 'cham), prop. masquerade, masked ball; Cs.: buffoonery, grimaces.

I. vb. pf. _obags ('bags), fut. dbag (dbag)? cf. sbág-pa (sbag pa), to defile, to pollute one's self, bud-méd (bud med) dañ (dang) with women Dzl.; _odod-čágs-la ('dod chags la) through lust Dzl.; to defile, to soil, to dirty, snód-la (snod la) a vessel Dzl. 222, 7? - 2. C. to take away, to steal, to rob; to covet, to wish to take, c. la Mil. (acc. to oral information).
a slight elevation, hillock W.
spider, _obag-rág-gi ('bag rag gi) tsañ (tshang) cob-web Sik.
rest, remainder, remnant (of food) Mil.
to be soaked, macerated, softened by soaking Cs., cf. sbáñ-ba (sbang ba).

subject, rgyál-po (rgyal po) _obáñs-su ('bangs su) _oòñ ('ong) the king turns into a subject Ma.; _obañs ('bangs) byéd-pa (byed pa) to obey, bkai (bka'i) _obañs ('bangs) bgyíd-par (bgyid par) (or bka-_obáñs-su (bka' 'bangs su)) k'as-blañs-so (khas blangs so) they promised to obey, to perform the command-ment Mil. frq.; báñs-su (bangs su) byéd-pa (byed pa) Cs. to reduce under one's dominion; gen. collectively: the people, the subjects, opp. to blón-po (blon po) officers, magistrates, or rje (rje), rgyál-po (rgyal po) etc. - lha-báñs (lha bangs) Tar. 165, 22 Schf.: slaves belonging to a temple.

I. vb., imp. _obod ('bod), to endeavour, to exert one's self, apply one's self, c. la or the termin.; dus-rgyún-du (dus rgyun du) čós-la (chos la) _obád-pa ('bad pa) de (de) this (habit of) constantly applying one's self to religion Mil.; also c acc.: dká-ba (dka' ba) brgya-p'rág (brgya phrag) to perform a hundred exercises of penance; col. to cultivate, raise, rear, take care of, žiñ (zhing) or sa-yži (sa gzhi) to cultivate the ground, rgun-_obrúm (rgun 'brum) to grow vines, dúd-_ogro (dud 'gro) to breed cattle; slob-par (slob par) to apply one's self to learning, gléñ-mo (gleng mo) k'ó-nar (kho nar) to devote one's self exclusively to public speaking, preaching C. - II. sbst.
